grammarAdaptation.description: No tenant-editable grammar surface. CloudEvent schemas, HMAC verification, and event names remain MACHINE.

roleScopeAdaptation.description: Tenant role isolation is fixed for certification. Tenants may not weaken admin/support boundaries or cross-tenant denial rules.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Tenant ETL operating policies that can vary without changing security, ordering, or fabric constraints.

edgeCases.name: High-volume sync
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A raises sync page and backfill slice sizes for bulk migration.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T214/T216 preserve rate checks, cursor monotonicity, raw append-only storage, and store-before-enqueue.

edgeCases.name: Strict schema governance
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A lowers drift threshold and shortens approval timeout.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T218 pauses on drift and emits SchemaDriftDetected before continuation.

edgeCases.name: Sensitive identity mode
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A raises identity confidence floor and lowers join input cap.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T221 still blocks cross-tenant inputs before persistence.

edgeCases.name: Reverse ETL batching
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A changes push batch size and lock TTL for a destination system.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T223 remains queue-fabric-only and rate-limited before lock check.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task type range remains T213 through T224.
All tenant-editable configuration keys use the flow14_ prefix.
Raw zone remains append-only with no update/delete path.
Connector credentials remain Vault-only and never appear in event payloads.
HMAC verification remains timing-safe and fail-closed.
FLOW-13 peer ACTIVE check remains required for T222.
Reverse ETL remains queue-fabric-only with no direct HTTP.
RLS is mandatory for mart, identity, cross-flow, and zone provisioning results.
storeDocument occurs before enqueue for all emitted workflow events.

tenantAHighVolumeMigration.description: Tenant A increases sync throughput for an initial migration.
tenantAHighVolumeMigration.impact: Bulk migration completes faster while rate checks, cursor monotonicity, and raw append-only behavior remain enforced.
tenantAHighVolumeMigration.testable: Tenant A adaptation evidence records the policy in tenant.config.json and fork CI still passes.

tenantAStrictGovernance.description: Tenant A lowers schema drift tolerance and raises identity confidence.
tenantAStrictGovernance.impact: More schema changes require approval and identity joins become more conservative without weakening tenant isolation.
tenantAStrictGovernance.testable: Before/after assertions show changed drift and confidence thresholds with 401/403 auth checks still passing.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Every FREEDOM key is flow-scoped with flow14_.
STEP-1 invariants list all configuration keys before Phase 1 reads them.
Tenant adaptations can change at least one FLOW-14 policy value without changing MACHINE rules.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing/cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
